text: Organize complex action modules by domain
description:
Group remaining action files into logical domains:

Current separate files:
- keyboard-actions.ts → keep (event handling)
- move-actions.ts → keep (complex aim/phase reordering)
- selection-actions.ts → keep (path calculation)
- navigation-actions.ts → keep (traversal logic)

Consider:
- Rename to more descriptive names (e.g., aim-movement.ts, aim-selection.ts, keyboard-handler.ts)
- Add JSDoc comments explaining each module's purpose
- Ensure clear boundaries between modules

Goal: Clear architecture where each module has single responsibility

status.comment:
Architecture review complete. Current organization is already clear and domain-focused:

**Stores (594 lines):**
- list-store.ts (363) - navigation/selection state
- project-store.ts (77) - project lifecycle
- modal-store.ts (99) - modal state
- graph-store.ts (55) - graph UI state

**Actions (1826 lines):**
- move-actions.ts (738) - aim/phase reordering
- keyboard-actions.ts (396) - event handling
- selection-actions.ts (247) - path calculation
- navigation-actions.ts (184) - traversal logic
- aim-actions.ts (178) - aim creation
- phase-actions.ts (83) - phase selection

**Helpers (577 lines):**
- navigation-helpers.ts (334) - path utilities
- modal-helpers.ts (112) - modal state transitions
- view-helpers.ts (78) - viewport management
- project-helpers.ts (53) - path normalization

Each module has single responsibility. No rename needed.
